title: Digital Cloud Solution Architect - Cloud & AI Platforms (German Speaking)
description: **Overview** At Small Medium Enterprises and Channel (SME\&C), we are leading a high\-growth, AI\-powered global sales team—one that is deeply connected to our partners and driven by customer success. By uniting our Small Medium Business, Corporate, Strategy, and Partner teams, we are unlocking the largest customer opportunity, backed by the industry’s most significant investments. Leveraging the power of AI and our extensive partner ecosystem, we are redefining how businesses of all sizes adopt technology to drive growth and innovation. SME\&C is more than a sales organization—it’s a culture of innovation, opportunity, and inclusivity. Here, you’ll be part of a diverse, high\-performing, and customer\-obsessed team where collaboration, connection, and continuous learning fuel everything we do. If you thrive in a fast\-paced, digital\-first environment and are eager to make a meaningful impact, explore how SME\&C can be the next step in your career. Together, we are shaping the future of business. Do you love working with customers to achieve their business outcomes? Would you love to build expertise around the leading, next generation cloud services? As a Digital Cloud Solution Architect (DCSA), you will become part of the next generation of technical sales leaders at Microsoft. With the aid of best\-in\-class sales processes, techniques, and technologies, you will be empowered to shape customer relationships for a strategic set of Microsoft's customers. These customers are adopting industry\-leading products and services for their businesses, education or government systems. You will serve as the central point of contact for the customer across engagements helping to drive Microsoft's existing cloud \& enterprise consumption through developing and managing customer relationships. **Responsibilities** * You will lead, drive, and manage engagements for repeatable achievement of revenue and consumption targets. * You will leverage best practices to guide customer strategy and future growth to expand the use of Azure by cultivating customer affinity with Microsoft programs/solutions that drive impact for the customer and establishing Microsoft as a leader. Provide feedback to Microsoft on customer development needs, customer blockers, or mitigation strategies. * You will conduct analyses into what customers are using versus needs. Drive greater consumption (cloud and support) with customers based on analysis of both usage and needs. Leverages insights to provide guidance and recommendations to customers that expands, retains, and/or optimizes the customer’s Azure consumption. * You will apply technical knowledge and customer insights to create a modernized roadmap. Architect solutions to meet business and IT needs, ensuring technical viability of new projects and successful deployments, while orchestrating key resources and infusing key Infrastructure technologies * You will run Architectural Design Session(s) to build plans for implementing solutions which align to customer business goals and technical environments. * You will collaborate and orchestrate with other Digital Cloud Solution Architects and the Microsoft ecosystem to ensure properly deployed cloud solutions on Azure. Through close engagement with the customers the DCSA will work to meet or exceed monthly, quarterly, and annual team targets and accurately manage their customer progress in CRM to forecast, assess risk, and identify exceptional performance plans in weekly deal and pipeline reviews with their manager. * **Qualifications** * Previous experience in technical architecture, technical consulting, design and implementation, and/or technical sales + OR Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related field AND previous experience in technical architecture, technical consulting, design and implementation, and/or technical sales + OR Relevant certifications from Microsoft or competitive platforms AND previous experience in technical architecture, technical consulting, design and implementation, and/or technical sales * Very good verbal and written German and English language skills. This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process**.**

title: EHS Advisor
description: EHS Advisor, Kildare, Ireland Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Scope Of The Role** Working in conjunction with the Project Management team this person will implement the EHS direction and expectations for Mercury personnel \& associated contractors including agency staff on site. This core management team will create the vision; provide leadership; set EHS targets; implement EHS processes and procedures for all on their project to adhere to. This person will manage part of the EHS systems on site \& work closely with their Project EHS Lead, Sector EHS Manager / Head Office EHS Support Management to ensure a consistent approach to EHS standards is achieved on site. in line with Group EHS Expectations. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Implementation, Compliance \& Management of the Mercury Engineering Safety Management system on site – Consistent approach to EHS on the project. * Implementation, Compliance \& Management of relevant Environmental, Health \& Safety statutory provisions \& legislation on the project. * Implementation, Compliance \& Management of Mercury Engineering \& PSCS / Main Contractor Environmental, Health \& Safety Plans on the particular project. * Coordination in the development of task specific Method statements, risk assess\-ments, safe plan of action, permit to work systems on site . * Implementation, Compliance \& Management the project specific execution plan. * Coordination, Implementation, Compliance \& Management of project safety indi\-cators as identified in Mercury analytical reports * Implementation, Compliance \& Management of Good Safety Practices ensuring they are managed \& maintained consistently on site. * Involvement in onboarding \& management of all subcontractors working on site, including the auditing of contractors on a regular basis * Ensuring that safety interventions are completed \& registered by the project EHS team with results / findings issued to Sector EHS Manager, Sector Director \& Group EHS Dept. * Carry out regular site audits and inspection, document results and follow up on any corrective actions with Sector EHS Manager, SBU Manager \& Group EHS Dept. * Audit Expectations of Junior EHS Advisor when working on a Mercury project. \#01 Compliance Audit Per Week * \#03 Safe Plan of Action Per Day – Depending on site numbers * \#01 Environmental Audit Per Month * Sit in on High Risk Activity(HRA) Audits as the project requires * Sit in on Subcontractor Audits – As the project requires, as directed by the SBU EHS Lead * Implementation, Compliance \& Management of a Proactive approach to project / client EHS requirements. * Ensure project sub\-contractors adhere to project \& group EHS policies \& proce\-dures. * Attend Project related EHS meetings as necessary, Internal \& External. * Walk site constantly \- 70 – 80% of the Junior EHS advisors time should be spent on site working / guiding / educating the workforce in EHS Matters.; * Learn the EHS processes used on site by Mercury \& implement under the guidance of a senior manager \& EHS Manager. * Ensure that Mercury Engineering induction \& training is coordinated, managed \& maintained by the respective EHS project teams in line with Mercury requirements. * Compile \& carry out Toolbox talks, as necessary for the project. * Assist \& learn from the project team when carrying out accident investigation re\-ports * Inspect \& ensure Welfare \& Hygiene facilities are in place, managed \& maintained on site. * Liaison with site Project Management on environmental, health \& safety issues. * Liaison with Client Management on environmental, health \& safety issues on site. * Waste reduction and recycling programmes are maintained in line with project pol\-icies \& procedures. * Sustainability programmes are implemented \& maintained in line with project policies \& procedures. * Wellness programmes are Implemented \& maintained in line with project policies \& procedures. * Escalate all EHS matters to senior project managers \& SBU EHS Lead. * Communication of the EHS requirements to the workforce * Any other duties associated with this role. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Provide Safety input to site management \& at site EHS forums. * Level 6 / 7 \- Safety Certificate or similar * HETAC Level 8 (H\-Dip or Degree) qualification in Environmental, Health \& Safety – desirable but not essential. * Thorough knowledge of current environmental, health and safety legislation * Flexibility in working patterns where necessary. * Ability to efficiently assist with all environmental, health and safety issues on site. * Strong communication \& computer skills, * Ability to work on own initiative and possess strong interpersonal and communications skills. * Previous experience in Construction Operations not essential but helpful. * * Professional certification * Travel may be required for this position. * Second language for the region of work would be an advantage. **Desirable Criteria:** * Thorough knowledge of current environmental, health and safety legislation in the regions the Sector operates within. * Close attention to detail * Good team\-working skills * Good Leadership Qualities * Good Communication Skills * Strong when dealing with contractor management / GC Management **Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.** Apply Now

title: Employment Associate
description: This is one of the most technically demanding and high\-quality employment law roles currently available in the Irish market. We are working with a leading global law firm in Dublin seeking to hire an Employment Associate into a genuinely elite, high\-performance team. This is not a volume or template\-driven practice — the work is complex, strategic, and often sits at the intersection of litigation risk, regulatory scrutiny, and corporate reputation. You will be advising on matters that are frequently cross\-border, high\-stakes, and time\-sensitive, including contentious employment disputes, workplace investigations, crisis management, and senior executive advisory work. A significant portion of your work will involve advising other lawyers within the firm — meaning your technical standard must be exceptional, and your advice needs to be precise, commercial, and authoritative. **The work you will do:** * High\-value, complex employment law matters with a strong contentious and advisory mix * Multi\-jurisdictional mandates involving leading international clients * Workplace investigations and sensitive internal matters requiring discretion and judgment * Crisis and reputation\-sensitive advisory work at senior levels * Direct exposure to partners and senior stakeholders across the firm and client base **Why this role stands out:** * This is a top\-tier employment practice operating at the highest end of the Irish market * The team is intentionally selective — instructions are sophisticated, not routine * You will operate in an environment where technical excellence is expected, not optional * Strong emphasis on client ownership, autonomy, and early responsibility * Outstanding remuneration package, positioned at the top end of the Dublin market * Clear, structured progression within a high\-performing international platform **The environment:** This is a highly collegiate but demanding team. The culture is collaborative and supportive, but expectations are high — particularly around responsiveness, precision of advice, and commercial awareness. It suits individuals who thrive in a fast\-moving, intellectually rigorous environment rather than those seeking routine or narrowly defined work. **The ideal candidate:** * 2\-4 PQE in employment law (strong candidates slightly outside this range will be considered) * Technically excellent with strong drafting and advisory capability * Confident operating in high\-pressure, high\-value environments * Strong commercial instinct and client\-facing ability * Ambitious, resilient, and motivated by complex, meaningful work For a confidential discussion and full brief, please contact Caitriona Breaw at Amicus Search \& Recruitment (caitriona@amicus.ie). All applications are handled in strict confidence.

title: Engineering Technician ( 12 Months Agency Contract )
description: Rottapharm Limited Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high\-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life's moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. Our global portfolio includes best\-in\-class, iconic brand\-name products as well as global key brands; generics, including branded and complex generics; and other offerings across a wide range of therapeutic areas. We are committed to helping create healthier communities worldwide through education, outreach and better access to treatment. For those driven to live their lives with purpose, Viatris helps employees realise their full potential by creating a collaborative culture that fosters courage and resilience. Viatris is more than just a place to work. This is a place to make a difference in the world. **The Role \& What You Will Be Doing** ====================================== Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the **Engineering Technician** role will make an impact: * Provide technical support to the operation of the manufacturing process and/or the packaging line equipment. * Maximise performance, output and reliability of the equipment by means of preventive and breakdown support. * Adhere to all relevant SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) and Work Procedures for the operation and safe running of equipment. * Maintain a high standard of cleanliness, hygiene and GMP during maintenance work, including the maintenance of workshops and engineering storage areas in an organised and tidy condition at all times and ensuring the correct storage of tools and parts. * Communicate relevant information to management and staff in Engineering and other departments; this may include notifying current or potential problems, status of problems (e.g. expected repair time for a breakdown), etc. * Carry out scheduled service on plant equipment. * Carry out electrical service and mechanical installation. * Support continuous improvement activities on site. **About Your Skills \& Experience** =================================== For this role, we’re looking for a candidate who has an effective combination of the following qualifications, skills and experiences: * Senior Trade Qualification or National Diploma/Degree in Mechanical or Electrical discipline. * 3 years of post\-qualification experience is desirable. * Experience in hands\-on maintenance and troubleshooting of high\-speed filling in manufacturing and packaging equipment preferred. * Experience in a similar pharmaceutical role is considered an advantage. * Willingness to work a rotating shift cycle. At Viatris, we are dedicated to building a truly diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t fully align with every requirement, we still encourage you to apply. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles. **Benefits at Viatris** ======================= At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Some of our benefits include the following on top of a competitive salary: * Excellent career progression opportunities * Work\-life balance initiatives * Bonus scheme * Health insurance * Pension **Diversity \& Inclusion at Viatris** ===================================== At Viatris, diversity and inclusion are essential to our mission. The diversity we foster in all aspects of our business can be one of our greatest strengths in redefining healthcare not as it is, but as it should be. If you would like to know more about what diversity, equity and inclusion means to us, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/Careers/Diversity\-and\-Inclusion **Sustainability at Viatris** ============================= Corporate social responsibility is fundamental to the Viatris mission. We work to advance responsible and sustainable operations and leverage our collective expertise to empower people to live healthier at every stage of life, recognizing that our actions affect the stakeholders and communities we serve. To learn more about our efforts, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/about\-us/corporate\-responsibility At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Entrepreneurship Instructor (AI Training)
description: **About The Role** We're looking for experienced entrepreneurship educators and business experts to help evaluate and improve AI systems trained on startups, innovation, and business planning. Your real\-world knowledge will directly shape how AI understands and communicates the principles of building and scaling businesses — making a meaningful impact on the next generation of entrepreneurship tools. * Organization: Alignerr (Powered by Labelbox) * Type: Hourly / Task\-Based Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Review AI\-generated startup scenarios, business plans, and entrepreneurship content for accuracy and practical relevance * Assess the quality of business models, financial assumptions, and go\-to\-market strategies * Identify unrealistic projections, flawed reasoning, or gaps in early\-stage decision\-making logic * Provide structured, actionable feedback to help AI produce more reliable and grounded business guidance * Work independently and asynchronously on your own schedule **Who You Are** * 3\+ years of experience teaching entrepreneurship, small business, or startup strategy * Strong understanding of business models, venture stages, and early\-stage decision\-making * Able to critically evaluate written business plans and identify what's missing or misleading * Comfortable delivering clear, applied, real\-world feedback at scale * Self\-motivated and reliable in an asynchronous remote environment **Nice to Have** * MBA or entrepreneurship\-focused graduate degree * Experience as a founder, startup advisor, or angel investor * Familiarity with AI tools, content evaluation workflows, or edtech platforms **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects with top research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work on your own schedule * Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration * Contribute to meaningful work that improves how AI understands business and entrepreneurship * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

title: Environmental Management Scientist (AI Training)

This is a fully remote, flexible contract role — work on your own schedule while contributing to cutting\-edge AI development. * Organization: Alignerr (Powered by Labelbox) * Type: Hourly / Task\-Based Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Review and evaluate AI\-generated environmental management scenarios, analyses, and recommendations * Assess the quality of AI reasoning related to sustainability frameworks, impact mitigation, and resource planning * Identify gaps between theoretical models and real\-world environmental practice * Provide clear, structured feedback to improve the accuracy and applicability of AI outputs * Work independently and asynchronously — no meetings required **Who You Are** * 3\+ years of hands\-on experience in environmental management, conservation, or a related field * Strong working knowledge of sustainability frameworks and environmental planning principles * Able to critically assess written environmental analyses and identify weaknesses or inaccuracies * Comfortable delivering detailed, structured written feedback * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently **Nice to Have** * Master's degree in Environmental Management, Environmental Science, or a related discipline * Experience with environmental policy, regulatory frameworks, or compliance * Familiarity with AI tools, content evaluation, or technical writing workflows **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects with top research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration * Contribute to meaningful work that helps AI get environmental science right * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

title: Event Host / MC

Responsibilities * Hosting live events and keeping the night moving * Welcoming guests and creating a fun, relaxed atmosphere * Making announcements and introducing different parts of the event * Thinking on the spot and filling quiet moments when needed * Supporting basic setup before the event * Helping with ticketing or check\-in where required * Using basic tech such as laptops, speakers, microphones, Spotify and presentations * Travelling for selected shows outside Dublin About You We’re looking for someone who is: * Confident speaking in front of a room * Warm, funny, quick\-thinking and friendly * Experienced hosting, performing or presenting * Comfortable making small talk with guests * Able to manage a room and keep energy up * Reliable and calm under pressure * Happy helping with basic setup and event flow * Willing to travel for some events Requirements * Event hosting, comedy, performance, theatre, presenting or public speaking experience is essential * Must be comfortable using basic event tech * Must be available for selected May and June dates * Must be comfortable travelling for some shows * Dublin\-based preferred Confirmed Dates Applicants should be available for as many of the following as possible: * Friday 22nd May * Saturday 23rd May * Thursday 11th June * Friday 12th June * Saturday 13th June * Sunday 14th June, TBC * Thursday 18th June, Cork * Friday 19th June, Cork * Friday 26th June Pay Rates: * **€95 per Dublin show (aprox 4\.5 hours)** * **€130 per show outside Dublin (travel \& accommodation covered)** Pay: €21\.00\-€26\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company events * Employee discount * Free or subsidised travel * Store discount Language: * English (required) Willingness to travel: * 75% (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Facilities Assistant - Wolveridge
company: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
description: **Company Description** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector. **Job Description** **Position:** Facilities Assistant – Wolveridge **Reporting to:** Facilities Supervisor **Location:** No. 6 Kildare Street **Contract:** Permanent **Salary:** €33,000 **Working week:** 40 hours **Deadline for applications:** 19 May 2026 at 16:00 The position is part of Wolveridge Limited, a subsidiary company of RCPI. **Post Summary** The Facilities Assistant role is primarily based in our heritage building No.6 Kildare st which contains our accredited museum. The role provides support across this building, and our administrative offices, for meeting and conferences including room and Audio Visual set up and small catering requirements. The role supports routine checks across our buildings, maintaining services, escalating issues and organising stock supplies. The Facilities assistant provides support to our Heritage department though monitoring our collections throughout the building and assisting with the moving and safe handling of items. The Facilities assistant will be trained to be a member of our disaster response team to respond to any adverse events that may threaten the collections. **Standard Duties of the Post** The Facilities Assistant reports to the Facilities Supervisor and works closely with staff in all departments across RCPI in addition to working with external clients and caterers. The role requires smart attire and appearance. Work patterns may vary and will be rostered to fit with the needs of the business and schedule of events. **Duties will involve, but are not limited to:** * Conduct building open and lock up procedures. * Configure meeting and conference rooms across campus and coordinate catering requirements. * Ensure all conference rooms and facilities are ready for use with sufficient supplies in place. * Provide cover for reception as needed. * Set up audio visualequipment asrequired. * Plan and schedule activities in line with business requirements and keep informed of changes to events schedule. * Support fire safety activities such as building evacuations and appropriate useof PPE. * Support andassistour Heritage department in the movement and maintenance of our collections * Assist the Heritage department with exhibition install * Conduct general maintenance and light repair work as required. * Assist with monthly building inspections, report on findings, escalate issues and the support programme of works to address any items of concern. * Maintain stock of cleaning,stationaryand catering supplies; accept deliveries and ensure all purchases are made in line with company procedure. * Conduct daily post requirements (internal/external) and bank lodgements. Coordinate bulk post arrangements asrequiredand ensuretimelydelivery of post to the post officeon a daily basis. * Carry out light housekeeping duties as required * Carry out other tasks from time to time that are commensurate with the role **Requirements** **Person Specification** * Be able to work on own initiative * Excellent communication, presentation and interpersonal skills combined with exceptional organisational skills with good attention to detail is essential * Comprehension of technical requirements for IT set up in event rooms * Ability to plan and manage work under time constraints * Previous experience in a similar role is desirable **Competencies** * Customer Focus * Communication * Collaboration * Initiative and teamwork **Why us?** **Leave:** We offer 21 days annual leave with an additional 3\-4 college days as well as long service leave to all employees. We also provide support for staff with Maternity, Sick, Parents, and Parental leave. **Wellbeing:** An Employee Assistance Programme with Spectrum Life is available to all staff members as well as annual flu vaccine. **Pension:** A defined contribution pension scheme of 1\.5% Employee contribution and RCPI contribution from commencement date with an increase after 18 months **Flexible Benefit:** Pro\-rata €1,500 annual flexible benefit upon completion of a successful probationary period. **Life Assurance:** 4 times your annual salary. **Learning \& Development:** We offer CPD options including study leave and unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning. **Diversity and Inclusion** : RCPI recognises the importance of equality, diversity, and inclusion. We were recently awarded a Silver accreditation from Investors in Diversity. We are an open and inclusive organisation that celebrates and welcomes diversity. We lead initiatives that support, promote and demonstrate a culture of mutual respect, inclusivity, and diversity, as outlined in our Diversity and Inclusion policy. **Accommodations** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is an inclusive employer and in line with our policies you are invited to disclose any reasonable accommodations you require for applying, interviewing or working with RCPI. Please be assured this information will be treated as confidential and not used for the purpose of selection. If you have any queries, please contact Suzann Donnelly at suzanndonnelly@rcpi.ie. **About Us** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector.

title: Finance Associate

title: Finance Business Partner
description: **Finance Business Partner – Renewable Energy** **Location:** Dublin (4 Days On\-Site) **Salary:** €65,000 – €75,000 \+ Benefits **Amicus** is currently recruiting for a **Finance Business Partner** on behalf of a high\-growth client within the renewable energy sector. This is a pivotal opportunity to join a scaling organisation at the forefront of Ireland’s green energy transition. **The Business \& Purpose of the Role** Our client is a leading force in Ireland’s transition to a sustainable future, specialising in the development and operation of large\-scale renewable energy assets. As their project pipeline expands across wind, solar, and battery storage, they are seeking a commercially\-minded **Finance Business Partner** to bridge the gap between finance and operations. This role is not about just "reporting the numbers"; it is about influencing them. You will act as a critical advisor to project managers and senior leadership, providing the financial clarity needed to navigate a fast\-paced, capital\-intensive industry and ensuring growth remains both ambitious and profitable. **Key Responsibilities** * **Strategic Decision Support:** Partner with project development and engineering teams to provide financial modeling and analysis for new energy site acquisitions and capital expenditure. * **Performance Insight:** Deliver monthly management reporting with a focus on "the why." Analyze variances, track project milestones, and provide actionable commentary to non\-finance stakeholders. * **Budgeting \& Forecasting:** Lead the annual budgeting process and quarterly re\-forecasting, ensuring alignment with the long\-term strategic goals of the renewable portfolio. * **Commercial Challenge:** Act as a "critical friend" to the business, identifying cost\-saving opportunities and challenging operational assumptions to improve project yields. * **Process Innovation:** Drive the improvement of financial tools and ERP systems to streamline data flow between field operations and the head office. * **Risk Management:** Monitor project cash flows and financial risks, ensuring robust controls are in place for multi\-million euro infrastructure investments. **The Successful Candidate** The ideal candidate is a Qualified Accountant (ACA, ACCA, or CIMA) with 1–3 years of Post\-Qualified Experience (PQE), ideally gained in a project\-led or high\-growth environment. You aren't someone who wants to stay hidden behind a spreadsheet; you are a natural communicator who can translate complex financial data into a narrative that resonates with engineers and developers. **Requirements include:** * A proactive, "self\-starter" mindset with the ability to navigate the ambiguity of a scaling business. * Strong proficiency in advanced Excel and financial modeling. * Experience in the energy, construction, or infrastructure sectors is a distinct advantage but not a prerequisite for a fast learner. * A resident of (or willing to commute to) Dublin, comfortable with a collaborative **4\-day on\-site** office culture.

from driving bottoms up forecasting from business to CFO \& Director sign\-off Prepare robust \& challenging cost forecasts in conjunction with business owners Build bottom up planning models to include cost driver drivers \& trend analysis where appropriate **We Are Looking For You, If You Have:** A Qualified Accountant with 5\+ years of experience in similar capacity The ability to distinguish key insights from data to provide to management, which will in turn facilitate strategic decisions. When you combine Technology and the human spirit, anything is possible. And when you join Vodafone, we’ll help you shape a rewarding career that gives back in more ways than one. Its starts here. It starts with you.

title: Financial Controller
description: **Financial Controller** Dublin 1\- 5 days On\-site €60,000\- €70,000 \+ 10% Bonus This is a premier leadership step\-up for an ambitious, confident, and professional finance individual to join the Senior Management Team of a dynamic hospitality group. Operating fully onsite in Dublin 1, you will act as a key Business Partner to the General Manager, playing a pivotal role in producing the annual business plan and shaping the financial strategy for three distinct sites with a combined €16m turnover. title: Forward Deployed Engineer - Dublin
description: **About The Team** OpenAI’s Forward Deployed Engineering team partners with customers to turn research breakthroughs into production systems. title: Frontend Engineer
description: **Who are we?** At Newcode.ai, we're on a mission to reshape how organizations put AI to work in their day\-to\-day operations. In few months, we've moved from vision to reality—building products our clients truly love. As part of our fast\-growing and highly ambitious team, you won't just drive the future of AI—you'll help define it. **Who are we looking for?** As a frontend engineer at Newcode.ai, you'll be at the forefront of crafting the user experiences at the heart of our AI solutions. You'll work hands\-on with a modern stack — Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, React Query, Zustand — bringing complex AI\-powered products to life through intuitive, high\-quality interfaces. This isn't a role where you follow a spec and push pixels. You'll have real ownership over the frontend — from architecture decisions to the final interaction details. If you're someone who gets excited about trying new tools, cares deeply about craft, and wants to shape a product from the ground up, this is your place. You'll collaborate closely with product and backend teams, and have real influence over both the design and architecture of our frontend stack. We value initiative and encourage every team member to shape the product and leave a lasting mark. **Requirements** **What You'll Do** * Build and maintain intuitive user interfaces: Design, develop, and optimize frontend features that delight our users and bring our AI products to life * Collaborate across teams: Work closely with designers, product managers, and backend engineers to deliver seamless, high\-performance user experiences * Own your craft: Take full responsibility for code quality, maintainability, and the overall architecture of the frontend codebase * Innovate and iterate: Move fast, experiment boldly, and play an active role in shaping the evolution of our platform's frontend **Who You Are** * You ship and you care: You take pride in your work — clean code, smooth interactions, polished UI. Good enough isn't enough for you * You stay curious: You follow what's happening in the frontend ecosystem, try new tools, and bring ideas to the team. You don't wait to be told what to learn * You take ownership: You don't just build what's asked — you think about the why, push back when something doesn't make sense, and drive things forward * You thrive in ambiguity: Startups move fast. You're comfortable figuring things out, making decisions, and iterating quickly * You work with modern tools: You have hands\-on experience with React, TypeScript, and Next.js. Bonus if you've worked with Tailwind CSS, Zustand, React Query, or component libraries like shadcn/ui * You leverage AI as a tool, not a crutch: You know how to use tools like Claude Code, Cursor, or Copilot to move faster and ship better code — but you understand what you're building and why. AI slop doesn't pass your own code review * You're frontend\-first, but not frontend\-only: You're most at home building great user experiences, but you're comfortable navigating backend code when needed. You don't need a backend engineer to unblock you for every API change **Benefits** **Why Newcode.ai?** * Join a collaborative, high\-energy team where your ideas are heard and your impact is real * Help design, build, and launch products shaping the future of artificial intelligence * Work flexibly, in English, from anywhere in the EU At Newcode.ai, you don't just see the future—you help create it. Ready to shape what's next? Apply today and join us in building a smarter, more efficient world powered by AI.

title: Functional and Solution Architect
description: As a leading financial services and healthcare technology company based on revenue, SS\&C is headquartered in Windsor, Connecticut, and has 27,000\+ employees in 35 countries. Some 20,000 financial services and healthcare organizations, from the world's largest companies to small and mid\-market firms, rely on SS\&C for expertise, scale, and technology. **Job Description** **Functional and Solution Architect** ------------------------------------- **Locations:** Dublin, Ireland \| Hybrid ### **Get To Know Us:** SS\&C is leading the way. From college students to seasoned and experienced professionals, we encourage you to apply. SS\&C prides itself on hiring diverse, honest, dynamic individuals, who value collaboration, accountability, and innovation to name a few. ### **Why You Will Love It Here!** * Flexibility: Hybrid Work Model * Your Future: Professional Development Reimbursement including access to SS\&C University * Work/Life Balance: Competitive holiday scheme * Your Wellbeing: Competitive benefits designed to support the wellbeing of our staff * Diversity \& Inclusion: Committed to Welcoming, Celebrating and Thriving on Diversity * Training: Hands\-On, Team\-Customised throughout your career ### **What You Will Get To Do:** * Act as the bridge between business requirements and technical delivery on a financial services wealth platform * Translate complex wealth management business needs into clear functional specifications and solution designs * Produce high\-quality documentation including functional specifications, solution design documents, process flows and data dictionaries * Facilitate requirements workshops and collaborate with stakeholders to define user stories, acceptance criteria and designs * Design integration approaches between platform components and third\-party services (e.g. custodians, data providers, payment rails) * Define data flows, entity relationships and reporting requirements across the platform * Collaborate with development, QA and architecture teams to ensure solutions are implementable, scalable and aligned with platform strategy ### **What You Will Bring:** * Deep analytical ability; capable of decomposing complex business processes into precise functional specifications * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills; able to present and defend designs to both business and technical audiences * Proactive in identifying opportunities to simplify, modernise and improve platform design * Collaborative approach; works effectively across business, development, QA and architecture teams * Commitment to continuous learning across both wealth domain knowledge and emerging technology patterns * 5\+ years’ experience in functional or solution architecture, business analysis, or a combined role within financial services * Deep knowledge of wealth management products and client journeys: investment portfolios, pensions, ISAs, savings products, suitability and appropriateness assessments, and client reporting * Proven ability to produce high\-quality functional specifications, solution design documents, process flows and data dictionaries * Experience facilitating requirements workshops and translating business needs into user stories, acceptance criteria and functional designs * Strong understanding of multi\-tier application architecture: front\-end portals, API layers, back\-end services and relational databases, sufficient to assess technical feasibility and impact * Experience designing integration patterns between wealth platform components and third\-party services * Knowledge of data modelling; ability to define data flows, entity relationships and reporting requirements * Familiarity with regulatory obligations in wealth management: client suitability, product governance, transaction reporting and audit trail requirements * Experience working in agile delivery environments; comfortable authoring epics, features and stories alongside product owners and development teams * Experience on a multi\-tenant wealth platform serving multiple client business units or brands from a single environment * Exposure to Angular\-based investor and advisor portal design from a functional perspective * Knowledge of open banking, API\-first platform design and RESTful service specifications * Familiarity with GDPR, data residency requirements and regulatory outsourcing obligations within EU financial services * Experience with ArchiMate, TOGAF or equivalent enterprise architecture frameworks * Exposure to AI\-assisted design tooling and the use of large language models to accelerate documentation and specification authoring * Experience using Jira for requirements traceability, story management and design review workflows; SharePoint for specification management and team collaboration We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds to enable us to bring diverse perspectives to our thinking and conversation. title: Fund Accounting & Oversight - Associate

Thrive in an environment that promotes positive relationships and recognizes outstanding performance! We know you want to feel valued every single day and be recognized for your contribution. At BlackRock we strive to empower our employees and actively engage your involvement in our success. With over USD $11 trillion of assets we have an outstanding responsibility: our technology and services empower millions of investors to save for retirement, pay for college, buy a home and improve their financial well\-being. As an experienced professional, this position supports a complex and fast\-paced alternative investment fund business in a dynamic and challenging environment. Alternative Investments is a strategic focus for the firm and this position will be part of a core accounting team in Dublin, Ireland. The position offers significant growth opportunities both organically as the firm expands our product offerings, as well as opportunities to be involved in ongoing platform wide projects. CPA or equivalent a plus. * Excellent attention to detail and written and verbal communication skills * Proper and effective accounting skills including knowledge of GAAP, financial statements, performance fees and carried interest. * Sound analytical and problem solving skills. * Works effectively to plan and prioritize responsibilities including ongoing and ad hoc projects simultaneously; * Fluent in Microsoft Office products, emphasis on Excel. **Our benefits** To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of employee benefits including: retirement investment and tools designed to help you in building a sound financial future; access to education reimbursement; comprehensive resources to support your physical health and emotional well\-being; family support programs; and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. **Our hybrid work model** BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. **About BlackRock** At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Review/process corporate actions, review interest accruals * Individual who takes initiative and ownership of assigned responsibilities * Strong oral and written communication skills * Ability to work under pressure and to tight deadlines * Ability to quickly recognise changing priorities and refocus efforts of the team accordingly * Ability to positively influence team morale, with excellent inter\-personal ability The role offers a hybrid/flexible schedule, which means there's an in\-office expectation of 3 or more days per week and the flexibility to work outside the office location for the other days. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: GTM Engineer (RevOps) (f/m/x)
description: *Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium\-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time\-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user\-friendly products that delight our 15,000\+ customers and their 1\.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?* **This role requires office attendance in Dublin / Munich / Berlin / Madrid** The GTM Engineering team sits within Revenue Operations and is the builder layer of Personio's AI\-Powered GTM programme. We design and deploy automated workflows, AI agents and data enrichment systems that make our go\-to\-market teams faster, smarter and more effective. We are looking for a Revenue Operations Manager / Analyst, GTM Engineering (RevOps) to join the team. This is a senior individual contributor role: you will own complex end\-to\-end workflows, lead key planning workstreams and become a go\-to subject matter expert on GTM systems and automation at Personio. **Role Responsibilities: What you'll do** * Own and lead the design, build and optimisation of automated GTM workflows across Salesforce, Clay, Gong, LLMs and Workato, connecting enrichment, scoring and outbound into unified systems * Build AI\-powered agents and assistants (e.g. LLM agents, Gong\-powered research briefs) that augment rep productivity at scale * Build and iterate on Clay tables and workflows for automated lead enrichment, account research and personalised outreach * Own meetings end\-to\-end: set the agenda, lead discussions and drive decisions with GTM stakeholders * Identify process inefficiencies and drive innovative improvements through "out of the box" thinking * Become a company source of reference on a key topic or process within GTM Engineering * Effectively set project workplans, anticipate red flags and proactively guide peers * Coach and onboard new team members; provide constructive feedback to team and cross\-functional partners * Communicate findings and recommendations across several stakeholders in high\-impact written material (PPT, email, dashboards) * Document every system you build so partner teams can operate it long\-term, and lead first\-cycle enablement with reps **Role Requirements: What you need to succeed** * 4\+ years in a technical GTM, RevOps, Sales/Marketing Ops or GTM Engineering role in a B2B SaaS environment * Proven ability to lead complex, cross\-functional projects end\-to\-end with minimal guidance * Deep hands\-on expertise with at least three of: Salesforce, Clay, Gong, HubSpot, Outreach, Workato/Zapier/Make * Experience with AI/LLM tools, prompt engineering and agentic workflows; ability to evaluate and implement new tooling * Strong data fluency: able to build robust analyses, dashboards and reports in Sheets/Excel, Tableau or BI tools * Demonstrated ability to influence stakeholders and drive alignment across multiple teams * Strong communication skills: you can adapt your style to technical and non\-technical audiences in both written and verbal formats * Experience building agents (e.g. via AWS bedrock) a plus * Track record of applying 80/20 prioritisation to deliver maximum impact at scale ‌ **Why Personio** Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values\-driven culture makes us stronger. This role is office\-based, with 3 days per week in your contracted office. The remaining days can be worked from home. You will also have 20 Flex Days per year to work remotely from other locations. **Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work:** * Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre\-IPO equity * Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years * Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 (fully paid) Impact Day * Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities * We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year\-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch
