title: IT System Administrator
description: As an IT Systems Administrator at DOCOsoft, you are the first line of support for our infrastructure, systems, and applications across all business areas. You will manage joiner, mover, and leaver processes, maintain service desk standards, and resolve issues for internal users, partners, and contractors. Your duties cover the entire Microsoft 365 stack and Azure, including endpoint management, collaboration tools, license tracking, directory services, and app provisioning. You’ll handle Intune and Autopilot builds, troubleshoot connectivity and access issues, and uphold documentation and compliance. Unresolved or complex cases are escalated per company protocol, ensuring smooth operations across our cloud and on\-premises environments. **Responsibilities** * Serve as the initial point of contact for infrastructure, systems, and application support across all business areas, providing L1 triage and resolution for user issues involving endpoints, collaboration tools, SaaS apps, and on\-premises services; escalate unresolved or complex cases according to company SOPs. * Manage the joiner, mover, and leaver processes by delivering Day 1 readiness for new employees, maintaining accurate records for staff transitions, ensuring timely deprovisioning, and upholding strict license management and hygiene. * Manage and monitor backup systems such as Veeam and Azure Backups, ensuring regular backup schedules are maintained and performing restoration tasks as needed to support business continuity. * Build and maintain endpoints using Microsoft Intune and Autopilot, ensuring that all devices are patched, compliant, and accurately tracked in the asset register. * Maintain high service desk standards by creating well\-categorized tickets, leveraging the knowledge base, and actively contributing to SOP and KB documentation. * Assist with procurement and RMA processes for peripherals and accessories, coordinating effectively with vendors and internal stakeholders. * Administer basic Azure and directory services, including group access, app assignment, portal checks, reporting, and triaging identity and access tickets. * Troubleshoot connectivity issues such as Wi\-Fi and VPN problems, verify DNS and DHCP settings, diagnose network issues, escalate cases as needed, and assist with firewall and access requests. * Handle routine operational tasks such as directory services, printer management, file share administration, remote access setup, and standard application provisioning, while ensuring documentation and compliance are maintained. **Key Attributes** * 3\+ years’ experience in a IT Systems / Support Administration role. * A degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field. * Experience working in Azure environment (AZ900 Certification is favourable) * Experience in automation, developing and implementing comprehensive workflows, particularly with tools such as PowerShell, Power automate, Microsoft Endpoint Manager, and Intune, is highly valued. * Strong end\-user expertise across various platforms and technologies like Remote Access, and Cloud\-based productivity applications such as O365 and OneDrive. * Highly motivated, with a self\-starting problem\-solving mindset. * A strong interest in the latest technology and keeping up to date with new systems, process and services which can bring benefits to the team and company. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a proven ability to engage effectively with both highly technical teams and less technical stakeholders. * Adept at bridging the gap between IT specialists and end\-users by tailoring explanations to suit the audience, ensuring that complex technical concepts are consistently translated into clear, actionable information. **Who We Are** DOCOsoft is a leading software and services provider to Lloyd’s of London and the broader London insurance market. Since our foundation, we have grown to become one of the leading insurance software specialists in the London Insurance Market. We are a growing team of over 95 colleagues based in Dublin, London, Tokyo, Portugal, Spain, India and Poland. **Here’s What We Have To Offer** DOCOsoft aspires to be a market leader in the technology sector, and we are always looking for new ways to approach projects or improve existing content. We look to hire people that will help us achieve this with hard work, enthusiasm and an expression of their own ideas. We offer our people the opportunity to impact our growing business\- everyone’s contribution matters! A team environment that is focused on the creation and delivery of great products for our clients. Exciting challenges to grow and enhance their skills and a competitive pay and benefits package including; * + 25 days Annual Leave, + Private pension, + Bonus scheme, + Private health, + Life assurance. **Equal Opportunity Employer** DOCOsoft is committed to building an inclusive and diverse team that represents a variety of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, and do not discriminate on the grounds of race, religion, gender, marital or family status, age, disability, sexual orientation, membership of the travelling community or any other basis as protected by applicable law. Should you require reasonable accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: IT Systems Administrator
description: ### **Overview** This is a technical role within the Enterprise Services team reporting to the I.T. Service Desk Team Lead. You will work with the system administration and managed service partner to support the operation, performance, and availability of on\-premises and cloud infrastructure and core services. The role will focus on ensuring stable service delivery across the hybrid environment and enterprise platforms, with responsibility for day\-to\-day operational support, incident management, and continual service improvement. As required, you may also contribute to and act as a technical stakeholder in I.T. initiatives and projects. ### **Experience** * Proven experience as a system administrator supporting server operating systems (Windows Server 2019 above). * Experience supporting infrastructure and workloads in cloud environments (Azure) is essential. * Strong experience in managing Active Directory (on\-premises) and Microsoft Entra\-ID (Azure), including identity lifecycle management, role\-based access control (RBAC), and Group Policy. * Proficiency in administering M365 services, to include Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Intune. * Experience working with monitoring tools and performance management of infrastructure and services. * Exposure to security tools and technologies (patch management, antivirus, firewalls) an advantage. ### **Role Responsibilities** ### **System Administration** * Daily administration and support of IT infrastructure including servers, virtual environments, and cloud platforms. * Monitor system performance and availability, proactively identifying and resolving issues impacting service delivery. * Review system health reports, troubleshoot issues, and coordinate resolution with internal teams and external partners. * Ensure optimal performance, capacity planning, and reliability of infrastructure services. * Assist with deployment and lifecycle management of Windows operating systems, applications, and updates using Microsoft tools (Autopilot, Intune). ### **Identity and Access Management (Active Directory / Entra)** * Administer Active Directory and Entra ID environments across hybrid infrastructure. * Manage user identities, access provisioning, and deprovisioning processes. * Implement and maintain role\-based access control (RBAC) aligned with business requirements. * Maintain and optimise Group Policy and identity\-related configurations. ### **M365 Administration** * Administer M365 services including Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive. * Monitor service health, troubleshoot issues, and perform ongoing maintenance and optimisation. ### **Incident and Service Management** * Act as an escalation point for the Level 1 Service Desk and wider IT teams. * Take ownership of incidents related to infrastructure and platform services and ensuring timely resolution. * Coordinate with internal teams and managed service providers during incident response. * Assist the Major Incident Manager with root cause analysis for major incidents and contribute to continuous improvement actions. * Maintain accurate documentation of incidents, resolutions, and system changes. ### **General** * Work closely with managed service partners to ensure service performance and availability targets are met, taking proactive steps or escalating to the Service Desk Team Lead where you think targets will be missed. * Contribute to operational improvements, automation, and standardisation across infrastructure services. * Prioritise and manage workloads to effectively meet operational and project deadlines.

title: IT Systems Administrator
description: We are recruiting for a IT Administrator for a leading IT service provider based in Dublin. This is a Hybrid role with 3 days a week on site. Specialising but not limited to Microsoft technologies, the purpose of this role it the design, build, support and maintenance of complex, high quality infrastructure solutions. This role acts as the primary interface between the Cyber Security Project Team and the IT Operations function, with a strong focus on delivering security\-driven infrastructure projects and architectural change. The position is responsible for the design, planning, analysis, and implementation of strategic security initiatives that enhance the organisation’s security posture while ensuring alignment with existing infrastructure standards and operational requirements. Working closely with internal and external stakeholders, the role shapes and delivers infrastructure and security architectures that meet business objectives, regulatory expectations, and service\-level, security, and availability requirements. * Become technical lead for Cyber Security project delivering Infrastructure related Security Initiatives on our critical 24x7 Operational Environment. * Develop and implement industry standard best practice infrastructure designs to ensure scalability, resilience, and security of infrastructure services to the daa business. * Vulnerability \& Security patch management * Produce high\-quality documentation to support Internal and external audits * Complete daily, weekly and/or quarterly System Administration tasks as and when necessary to ensure that the environment remains in a healthy state. * Using industry and company best practice and information, provide improvement and efficiency suggestions to existing team procedures and processes on an ongoing basis * Using industry \& corporate best practices \& standards, design and build complex Microsoft related infrastructure environments with an emphasis on security hardening * Implement high\-availability and disaster\-recovery strategies for critical applications. * Collaborate with application teams to ensure performance, resilience, and secure traffic flows. * Resolve technical issues upon escalation from first and second level support. * Mentor first and second line staff to assist their development and help them contribute to team deliverables. * Provide Infrastructure and security performance monitoring – to ensure critical components are monitored to meet our SLA’s. * Continuous development of plans for failover and testing title: Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist
description: **Job Title: Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist** **Employer:** Civil Service **County:** Dublin \- Nationwide **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 21st May 2026 **Starting Salary:** €38,419 **About the Employer:** The Civil Service employs over 50,000 people nationwide across the 18 Government Departments and more than 20 other Civil Service offices and agencies. Our vision is inclusive, high quality and integrated service provision that meets the needs and improves the lives of the people of Ireland. Having the right people in place is essential if we are to deliver on the vision for Ireland to be recognised as a global leader in digital government. In this recruitment competition at Executive Officer (EO) level for Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist we are offering you an exciting chance to be a part of delivering on this vision. Candidates should be aware that the office locations for the vast majority of vacancies likely to arise in Civil Service organisations will be in the wider Dublin area. **About the Role:** As a core part of the digital and ICT function of a Civil Service organisation, the role of Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist is an important one. Infrastructure and Operations Specialists will generally work as part of a multi\-disciplinary digital/ ICT/digital team to design and deliver ICT/digital services for members of the public, businesses and colleagues across their own organisation. The specific duties of each Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist role will largely be determined by the Civil Service organisation to which you are assigned. However, some duties and responsibilities will be common to all Infrastructure and Operations roles such as: • Assisting with administration, implementation, diagnostics, and on\-going support of your organisation’s ICT infrastructure across a broad set of technologies and solutions; • Supporting the management of your organisation’s corporate ICT services to end users such as office productivity applications, email, on\-line collaboration and videoconferencing, access to internet and online services etc; • Assisting management in developing test strategies and test cases and carrying out appropriate testing during the introduction of new solutions; • Supporting end\-to\-end ICT infrastructure and operations solutions across a broad set of leading technologies; • Playing an active role in technical implementation projects and rollout of new hardware, applications and operating systems and ICT/digital services across your organisation which can include developing and providing hands\-on training and demonstrations; **About You:** We are looking for people who are enthusiastic, logical and imaginative, who want to begin their ICT career as part of the Civil Service and are eager to learn and develop new skills. **Do you have:** • strong technical aptitude and see opportunities for digital and ICT to deliver world class government services to citizens and businesses? • good communication skills and are you comfortable working as part of a team or in project groups? **Are you:** • someone who also takes a thorough and structured approach to organising tasks and solving problems and who can deliver on time against project deadlines? • someone who can establish and maintain good working relationships with others, identify their needs and be receptive and responsive to their queries? • confident and persuasive and able to explain complex ideas to a non\-technical audience in a structured and concise manner? • someone who keeps up to date with emerging technologies and is always eager to learn new skills? • happy working in an environment where there is ongoing and significant change? • focused on providing the best possible service to the public and to customers in your organisation? **If so, then we have a role for you!** The Closing Date for this competition is Thursday, 21st of May at 3pm. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

· Performance based annual bonus between 5\-10% · Employee Assistance Programme · Access to free Learning Platform · Bike To Work Scheme **Key Responsibilities** **Technical \& Engineering Support** * Review mechanical drawings, specifications, and submittals to ensure accuracy, coordination, and compliance with project requirements * Assist in developing mechanical design solutions and resolving technical queries * Support procurement by reviewing equipment selections, data sheets, and material approvals * Ensure that all mechanical installations comply with Standards as well as project specifications **Site Coordination \& Installation Oversight** * Monitor and inspect mechanical installations on site, ensuring quality, safety, and compliance with the design * Coordinate with subcontractors, suppliers, and other engineering disciplines to resolve issues and maintain efficient workflow * Attend site meetings, toolbox talks, and coordination workshops as required * Track installation progress and report updates to the Project Manager * Support the development of method statements and risk assessments **Quality Assurance \& Documentation** * Maintain accurate documentation including inspection test plans (ITPs), quality records, and red\-line drawings * Assist with equipment tagging, asset registers, and compliance documentation * Manage technical submittals, RFIs, and design clarifications * Ensure all installation records meet project and client requirements **Testing, Commissioning \& Handover** * Support commissioning teams to ensure systems are tested, balanced, and verified in line with specifications * Assist in preparing O\&M manuals, handover documentation, and as\-built information * Ensure seamless transition from construction to commissioning and handover phases **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Promote and ensure adherence to site safety standards and project\-specific H\&S requirements. * Liaise with the safety team to address site hazards, unsafe practices, and compliance issues. * Ensure all work follows the project’s Integrated Management System (IMS) and ISO standards **Skills \& Competencies** * Strong technical knowledge of mechanical building services (HVAC, piping, chilled water, heating, ventilation, BMS integration, fire protection systems, etc.) * Excellent technical drawing interpretation and problem\-solving skills * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, multinational construction environment * Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to coordinate across multiple teams * Proficient in MS Office and BIM tools (Navisworks, Revit) or similar * Highly organised with a methodical approach to documentation and reporting * Ability to work independently and with team on site . **Experience \& Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent qualification * **1\-2 years’** experience in mechanical building services or construction engineering\- * Experience working on large commercial, industrial, data centre, or pharmaceutical projects * Knowledge of Irish and EU construction standards and regulations beneficial

title: LDA - Project Planner
description: **Your new company** We are currently assisting the Land Development Agency with their recruitment processes. The Land Development Agency (LDA) is the State's affordable housing delivery body. Its main role is to acquire and develop State and other land to deliver affordable homes, supporting the creation of thriving communities and delivering ongoing positive social impact. It is overseen by an independent Board of Directors and has total capitalisation of up to €8\.75bn. To date €6\.25 billion of equity capital has been committed by Government and a further €2\.5bn capitalisation is permitted under legislation from other sources/debt. The LDA is active on more than 20 State Land sites and has a delivery pipeline in excess of 25,000 homes. The Agency also works in partnership with the country's largest and most experienced homebuilders and will deliver a further 8,000 homes under its Project Tosaigh initiative. Additionally, the LDA is working on large\-scale longer\-term strategic areas by master\-planning and bringing forward planning applications in locations such as Limerick Colbert Quarter, the Digital Hub in Dublin and Sandy Road in Galway. **Your new role** The Project Planner will be a key member of the Construction Team and offers a fantastic opportunity to support the management of complex residential and regeneration projects through the development life cycle. The successful candidate will assist in the creation, maintenance, and analysis of project schedules, ensuring the highest standards of time management and logical integrity in accordance with industry best practices. **Key Responsibilities** * Responsible for the active monitoring of projects, including the preparation and delivery of detailed progress reports for Internal stakeholders and the senior management team across the full development lifecycle, including Feasibility, Design, Statutory Planning, Procurement, Construction, and Handover. * Assist in establishing and maintaining standards and documentation to ensure consistency in Contractor/Developer programmes across the Agency portfolio. * Conduct regular site engagements to gather live progress data, ensuring the accuracy of the master schedule. * Prepare clear, data\-driven progress reports, dashboards, and KPIs for Internal Stakeholders and the Senior Management team. * Identifying programme risks and opportunities, supporting the Construction Manager in applying mitigation measures. * Provide technical support for change management, assessing the impact of design variations or delays on the master * schedule. * Assist in facilitating planning workshops with project teams and professional consultants to drive consistency in scheduling methodology. * Promote a proactive planning culture by maintaining professional relationships with both internal teams and external technical consultants. * Perform regular Integrity Checks on schedules to identify hard constraints, out\-of\-sequence progress, logic failures, etc. * Maintain accurate project registers, delay logs, and audit trails of schedule changes for record\-keeping purposes. **What you'll need to succeed** * Analytical Mindset: Exceptional attention to detail with a focus on data integrity and logical consistency. * Proactive Solution Finder: Driven and results\-focused, with the ability to identify schedule risks early. * Communication: Excellent written and oral communication skills for delivering technical reports to diverse stakeholders. * Autonomy: Ability to work unsupervised while remaining an integral part of a well\-defined project organisation structure. * Adaptability: Flexible approach to managing multiple project streams in a fast\-paced environment. **What you'll get in return** This is a unique opportunity to join the Land Development Agency, that's leading the delivery of some of Ireland's most critical infrastructure over the coming decade. You'll be part of a purpose\-driven team that values excellence, continuous learning, and long\-term career development. You'll collaborate with experienced professionals across construction, commercial strategy, and property development, all working together to create meaningful impact. The role offers a competitive salary depending on experience, along with hybrid working arrangements to support work\-life balance. This role and organisation can offer huge career growth and progression opportunities. Standard working hours are 40 per week, Monday to Friday, with flexibility where appropriate. Additional benefits include a discretionary performance bonus, employer pension contributions, and significant opportunities for career progression within a fast\-growing organisation. This is more than just a job \- it's a long\-term career opportunity with real purpose and national significance. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. Hays Ireland is a trading division of Hays Specialist Recruitment (Ireland) Limited and acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and employment business for the supply of temporary workers. By applying for this job you accept the T\&C's, Privacy Policy and Disclaimers which can be found at hays.co.uk

title: Lab IT Administrator (Dublin / Swords)
description: ALGOTEQUE is an IT consultancy firm that helps startups, mid\-sized and large corporations to create and deliver innovative technologies. Our team has a successful track record in designing, developing, implementing, and integrating software solutions (AI, ML, BI, Web, Automation) for Telecom, Energy, Bank, Insurance, Pharma, Automotive, Industry, e\-commerce. We deliver our services both in fixed\-price and time\-and\-materials models, helping our customers achieve their business and IT strategies. **Job Description** We are looking for a Lab IT Administrator to support GxP\-controlled lab IT systems, ensuring reliable operations, regulatory compliance, and end\-user support in a pharmaceutical environment. Location: Ireland \- Dublin / Swords. **Responsibilities** * Provide end\-user support and administration for lab equipment, servers, desktops, and software. * Troubleshoot issues across software, hardware, networks, and scientific instruments. * Perform system monitoring, maintenance, backups, and user access management. * Author and maintain CSV documentation (URS, FS/CS, IQ/OQ/PQ, test scripts, SOPs). * Ensure compliance with 21 CFR Part 11, EU Annex 11, and Data Integrity standards. * Collaborate with CSV Lead, System Integrators, Site IT, and Operations. * Act as SME for lab IT systems, supporting audits and inspections. **Profile / Requirements** * Bachelor's degree (or higher) in a technical discipline. * Professional experience in IT / digital roles. * Strong communication skills (written and verbal). * Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities. * Proven experience identifying operational issues and implementing effective solutions. **Nice To Have** * Experience in a GxP\-regulated environment. * Hands\-on experience with Pharmaceutical QC Lab standalone COTS systems. * End\-to\-end experience with CSV lifecycle documentation, including system use and administration procedures. * Solid understanding of Data Integrity principles. * Experience supporting audits and regulatory inspections. AO3980

title: Language Expert with Castilian Spanish
description: **Location : Dublin** **Work Model :** **Hybrid** **Job Summary** You are a key member of the Language Experts group closely monitoring and evaluating responses quality for your locale. As a Language Expert you evaluate the performance of the language model ensuring that it: Correctly understands user requests. Provides relevant and useful responses. Presents the information in a clear and understandable manner. You also review and evaluate ratings. **Responsibilities** Collaborating with the cross\-locale team of Language Experts you transform task\-specific challenges and observations into valuable insights and process improvement suggestions. You also identify and call out what works well. * LLM model Improvement and Accountability: Linguists are directly responsible for driving model enhancements specific to their respective language and partner with Subject Matter Experts to ensure that the model delivers high\-quality responses. * Data Analysis and Evaluation: Linguists meticulously analyze evaluation data to identify patterns successes and areas for improvement across various releases. * Evaluations Trustworthiness: Linguists safeguard the reliability of evaluations by vetting samples and assessing their quality. * Contextualized Guidance: Linguists create language\-specific evaluation guidelines and instructions to provide tailored support and enhance model performance. * Quality Control: They provide feedback and conduct quality assessments on evaluations and rewrites ensuring that human\-generated data aligns with the linguistic nuances and expectations of each language. **Skills And Qualifications** * Bachelors degree in Linguistics and in\-depth knowledge of language structures phonetics syntax semantics and language analysis methods. Advanced degrees (Masters or Ph.D.) in Linguistics preferred * Native\-level fluency in the target language is essential with exceptional command of grammar vocabulary and cultural nuances. * Strong analytical abilities to dissect language structures identify patterns and draw conclusions about language use. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills are necessary for presenting research findings and writing reports. * Meticulous attention to language details including pronunciation grammar and subtle language variations. * Understanding of cultural context social norms and non\-verbal communication associated with a language * Research skills: Ability to conduct research collect data and analyze linguistic phenomena is preferred. (not required) Programming languages (e.g. Python): Knowledge of programming for computational linguistics natural language processing and data analysis tasks is preferred

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Legal Secretary/Executive (General Litigation/Personal Injury)

title: Logistics Associate
description: **About the job** **Who we are:** EOS IT Solutions is a Global Technology and Logistics company, providing Collaboration and Business IT Support services to some of the world's largest industry leaders, delivering forward\-thinking solutions based on multi\-domain architecture. Customer satisfaction and commitment to superior quality of service are our top business priorities, along with investing in and supporting our partners and employees. We are a true International IT provider and are proud to deliver our services through global simplicity with trusted transparency. **Role:** Logistics Associate **What You'll Do:** * Be part of the IT Logistics operations team working on the delivery of day\-to\-day operations, deliver excellent service, accuracy and cost KPI's. * Take a hands\-on approach to work as well as co\-ordinating and completing tasks with the team. * Ensure exceptional performance levels on site across all areas of the operation including, but not restricted to quoting, bookings, tracking, compliance and managing vendors. * Complete all tasks efficiently and effectively. * Assist with cross functional teams to provide the best possible service and cost to our customers. * Lead the team to ensure targets outlined by IT Logistics Business Plans are met, including delivery of KPI's, values and people processes. * Support the IT Logistics teams to attract, acquire, retain and develop the necessary talent great service at the best possible cost for EOS and our partners. * Play a key part on delivering best in class operations and share best practice internally with the EOS teams. * Seek out, actively We invite you to consider opportunities at EOS regardless of your gender, gender identity, gender reassignment, age, religious or similar philosophical belief, race, national origin, political opinion, sexual orientation, disability, marital or civil partnership status, or other non\-merit factor. \#LI\-Onsite \#LI\-CT2

title: Logistics Associate
description: **About \&Open** We’re the global gifting platform for companies that care. Our platform sends gifts globally at scale, while our team guides the process with taste, thought and attention to detail. As a B Corp\-certified company, we’re all about less waste, more impact. At the end of the day, it’s not how much you send, it’s how you make people feel that matters. Our customers include OpenAI, Hubspot, Revolut and Intercom. Come and grow with us. **About The Team** Our Logistics team is growing! We’re obsessed with enabling the delivery of gifts to people across the globe, and work to create the best possible solutions for our customers and the people they wish to gift. To make this all happen, we collaborate closely to understand each client's needs and find the most effective methods to surprise and delight the lucky gift recipients. We care deeply about the journey of our gifts, carefully planning every step from source to doorstep. **About The Role** Come and join our logistics team to support fulfilment operations in our Dublin warehouse. This is an active role that combines hands\-on warehouse work — sorting, packing and managing stock — with system\-side responsibilities such as monitoring our WMS, coordinating delivery exceptions and supporting wider operational initiatives. **What you'll do?** * Pick, pack and dispatch outbound shipments accurately, across a range of sizes, formats and gift types. * Receive, check and book inbound shipments into the Warehouse Management System (WMS), ensuring accuracy and timely stock availability. * Work as a team to ensure all orders are shipped within the SLA and contribute towards the successful achievement of targets. * Manage the flow of goods through our Dublin studio warehouse. * Support the planning and execution of large operational initiatives such as stock moves, warehouse changes and ongoing process improvements across the logistics function. * Monitor and analyse in orders across our fulfilment partners to identify discrepancies, delays and fulfilment risks. * Coordinate delivery exception for shipments in transit by identifying issues and supporting investigations and solutions. * Perform system and administrative updates to ensure data accuracy across operational tools. * Assist with other ad\-hoc tasks required to support the day\-to\-day running of Logistics operations. * Support the wider Logistics team by providing cover during holidays, busy periods and team absences. **What You'll Need** * Fluent written and spoken English is essential. * Experience of working in a customer focused environment. * 1–3 years of experience in a warehouse, fulfilment, or logistics environment. * Comfortable with the physical demands of the role: standing for extended periods, lifting and moving boxes, and working in an active warehouse environment. * Experience working with a WMS or similar inventory/order management system is a strong plus. * Exceptional attention to detail, a positive can\-do attitude, time management, and organisational skills. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to tight timelines. **Benefits** * Competitive Salary * Company Pension * Healthcare cover * 25 days Annual Leave \+ 2 Company Days * Maternity and Paternity Paid Leave * Exciting experience in a fast\-paced start\-up environment * Employee Stock Options Plan **Location** Head Office: Dublin, Harold's Cross **All Welcome Here Statement** We believe diverse teammates, opinions and backgrounds generate a larger global impact and more considerate acts of care. \&Open is an equal opportunity employer and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status.

title: MERCHANDISER - ROI
description: LEARN MORE ABOUT US AND THE ROLE:**Location:** Ballybrack (A96 CD72, D18 F5F7\) **Hours:** 5\.5 hours per week **Days:** Monday \& Friday **Benefits:** Holiday Entitlement, Merchandiser Recognition Programme, Employee Benefits Scheme, Expenses/Mileage/Travel Time where applicable As an inclusive employer Carlton Greetings are passionate about creating a workforce that represents the diversity of our communities and the customers we supply. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for this flexible and customer focused role where every day offers fresh challenges; from recognising new sales/merchandising opportunities, to displaying brand new ranges. As a part time Greeting Card Merchandiser you will be the face of Carlton Greetings, calling upon retail outlets in and around your local area, ensuring our Customers’ displays are of the highest standard. **The role involves:** * Visiting local retailers, high street chains or supermarkets * Tidying \& filling greeting card \& gift dressing displays * Locating \& unpacking deliveries for replenishment of displays * Installation of new displays * No cold calling or direct selling is involved **To be a Part Time Merchandiser, you will need to have:** * Reliable \& conscientious work ethic * Flexibility around working times \& notably during peak seasonal periods when overtime is likely to be required * The initiative \& ability to work on your own * A strong team ethic * A confident \& friendly personality * No experience is required, as we offer full training * Full driving licence (desirable) So if you love to get out and about, enjoy meeting new people and would like to be part of a great team that promotes and values diversity, then this may be the role for you! WHO WE ARE At Carlton Greetings we are passionate about creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity. We help make the world a more thoughtful and caring place every single day with our greeting cards, gift dressings and social expressions products created for life’s big events, special occasions and those “saw\-this\-and\-thought\-of\-you” moments of life. Along with our sister Company John Sands, we’re part of the American Greetings Corporation, one of the largest privately owned greeting card publishers in the world. We’re one of the largest direct to retail publisher of greeting cards and social expression products in the UK \- so you’ll find our amazing products just about everywhere! **Here at Carlton Greetings we take pride in being an inclusive and diverse employer \- we** **are dedicated and committed to building a culture where difference is valued and** **everyone is able to** **fulfil** **their potential at work regardless of their background.**

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Hollyfort, a private company specialising in public works for Government Bodies, including Healthcare and County Councils, is expanding! We're seeking multiple Maintenance Technicians to join our growing team to work on exciting projects throughout Dublin and Leinster. We’re looking for a practical, hands\-on Maintenance Technician who can handle a wide variety of household repair and improvement jobs. If you’re someone who takes pride in fixing things properly and leaving customers happy, this could be the role for you. **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Visiting homes, commercial properties, and public spaces to carry out maintenance and repairs. * Performing a wide range of tasks, including painting, patch repairs, tiling (preferred but not required), and general upkeep. * Undertaking and resolving maintenance problems to a basic level across all disciplines * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance to ensure properties remain in top condition. * Collaborating with tradespeople, general operatives, and subcontractors to complete projects efficiently. * Keeping accurate records of maintenance checks and documenting all work carried out. * Providing a friendly, reliable service that clients and customers can trust. * Proper and safely use of hand and power tools **What We’re Looking For:** * Versatile practical skills across domestic maintenance and repair work. * Experience with painting and decorating, patch repairs, and general upkeep. * Tiling, carpentry, or minor plumbing skills would be a bonus. * A hands\-on, problem\-solving approach and the ability to handle a variety of tasks. * Reliability, good communication, and pride in delivering quality work. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Valid driving license **Why Join Hollyfort?** * Every day is different – no two projects or jobs are the same. * Excellent opportunity to grow and develop * We value skill, initiative, and the satisfaction of a job well done. * You’ll have the chance to use your versatility to make a real difference for our customers. title: Management Accountant
description: Job Title: Management Accountant **About Activ8 Solar Energies** Activ8 Solar Energies are actively seeking a Management Accountant to join our Finance team at our headquarters in Carrickmacross, Co. Monaghan. This is a full\-time permanent position inclusive of a competitive salary, bonus, pension, healthcare and additional benefits. We're dedicated to empowering homeowners and businesses towards self\-sustainability through exceptional customer experiences and innovative products. Be part of an industry that's shaping the future of our planet. Why Join Us? **About** At Activ8, we’re not just about energy efficient upgrades, we’re about people. You’ll join a team that values: * Quality – We don’t just meet standards, we set them. * Innovation – It’s in our DNA. * Respect \& Inclusion – Caring for our people fuels our success. * Accountability – We say what we do, and we do what we say. * Safety – If it is not safe, we don’t do it. The Opportunity As a Management Accountant, you will form part of the in\-house Finance team across a broad range of accounting and reporting activities in a fast\-paced renewable energy environment. This role will be focused on the Commercial business unit within the organisation and suit someone who enjoys ownership of tasks, working cross\-functionally, and contributing to continuous improvement within an evolving business. Responsibilities Include * Prepare and maintain accurate accounting records and working papers in line with company procedures and accounting standards. * Preparation of monthly management accounts, including journals, accruals, and prepayments. * Analyse project performance and variances against budget and highlight trends. * Act as a finance business partner to the Sales and Commercial teams. Be comfortable in analysing project contracts to identify risks, cashflow implications, pricing models, service levels and performance obligations * Lead and support budget and forecasting processes. * Prepare and submit VAT, VAT RTD, RCT and CIS returns in a timely and compliant manner. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to support financial decision\-making. * Ensure financial data integrity across accounting systems and support system improvements where required. * Adhere to, and help develop, internal financial controls, policies, and procedures. * Undertake ad hoc finance and reporting tasks as required to support the growing business. **Qualifications And Skills** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience in a similar accounting or finance role, within an in\-house or industry environment. * Qualified Accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) * Experience/knowledge of multi country EU tax is advantageous * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal. * Excellent organisational skills with strong attention to detail * Ability to prepare management accounts for reporting purposes together with cost analysis back up * Self\-motivated with the ability to manage workload independently and take ownership of tasks. * Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines in a dynamic environment. * Proficient in Microsoft Office, with advanced Excel skills. * Experience with Sage Accounts, Intact IQ and Salesforce is advantageous. * Office based role so must live within a commutable distance of our HQ in Carrickmacross. Our Benefits * Competitive Salary. * Pension Contribution. * Laya Healthcare. * Gym Memberships. * Breakfast Bar. * Flexitime. * Plus, additional benefits. How to Apply Interested in making a difference in renewable energy while advancing your finance career? Apply through the link provided. Activ8 Solar Energies is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Managing Consultant
description: *At Fruition Group Ireland we embrace our values of* ***Pride*** *,* ***Energy*** *and* ***Perseverance*** *and our colleagues embed them as the foundation of their work. Our colleagues are proud to work for Fruition Group, they are pro\-active with high energy to maintain our market\-leading status. With a diverse team we recognise that no two people are the same and we focus on results rather than unachievable KPIs and work collaboratively to achieve our goals whilst having fun along the way!* *We are a high growth organisation who work with a huge range of technology employers throughout the UK, Ireland, EU and USA and as a business we re\-invest profits to ensure we are leading in recruitment innovation in our marketplace and equipping our colleagues with the best environment, tools and training to help them succeed.* *Following the great success and growth for our team in Bray we are now opening a new office in Dublin City! This is a great opportunity to join a team on a* ***Managing*** *level with possibilities to lead and be part of a team with both current and new colleagues!* ***The role*** Reporting directly into the Directors, who are some of Irelands biggest billers. You will play a pivotal part in the resourcing of candidates for our clients and the development of new client relationships. You will continue to develop your network of candidates within the tech (perm and contract) space and continually keeping up to date with current trends. You will think outside of the box for sourcing strategies and utilise all internal tech provided to maximise efficiency. There is excellent opportunity to be part of a new office with a successful current business that is rapidly growing globally. Where you get the chance to develop your mentoring and leadership skills, as you will begin to support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment. ***Key Responsibilities*** * Development of accounts into key accounts * Searching for suitable Tech candidates who match our clients’ requirements * Headhunting and networking on sites such as LinkedIn / GitHub / Stack Overflow * Writing and posting job adverts * Telephone interviewing candidates to assess their suitability for specific roles * Arranging interviews for candidates and liaising with them during this time * Making job offers to suitable candidates and handling rejections * Taking references / collating relevant supporting documents * Building relationships with candidates to create a strong talent pipeline * Use social media to create a personal brand * Keep up to date with trends within the Tech industry * Building relationships with existing and future clients * Utilise internal tech such as SourceWhale, LinkedHelper and Paiger * Ensure all data is accurately recorded on CRM * Attending meeting with clients to explore potential opportunities * Negotiating fees and terms of engagement * Manage the team \& support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment ***Key Skills and Capabilities*** * Outstanding communicational skills with the ability to adapt language to different audiences * Ability to build long—lasting professional relationships * Ability to handle difficult conversations * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills, attention to detail; well organised * Proactive and independent thinker * Excellent customer service skills * Strong presentation skills with the ability to convey key messages to diverse and wide audiences * Exceptional Stakeholder Engagement skills * Experience working to targets * Great negotiating skills * Excellent Experience using the telephone in a professional environment is desirable * Great understanding of technical recruitment * Experience within a sales environment is desirable ***Your Benefits to name a few:*** * **Competitive basic salary with uncapped earning potential** * **Lucrative commission – no thresholds!** * **Ongoing training with external learning providers** * **Clear and structured Career progression** * **Biannual winners trip to European destinations such as Marbella, Barcelona \& Amsterdam** * **Flexible working offer \- between the office and home** * **Quarterly lunch clubs for top achievers** * **Quarterly team socials** * **Friday early finish** * **Private healthcare** * **Pension Contribution** If you’re interested in joining Fruition as Managing Consultant, please get in touch for an initial confidential chat about our available opportunities, and we’ll happily share our story with you. For further information please email lois.gooding@fruitiongroup.com We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age.

title: Marketing Campaign Contractor - Spanish Speaking
description: **Marketing Campaign Contractor Southern Europe – Spanish Speaker** *Contract Role • Hybrid / In\-Office / Remote* **About Rippling** Rippling gives businesses one place to run HR, IT, and Finance. It brings together all of the workforce systems that are normally scattered across a company, like payroll, expenses, benefits, and computers. For the first time ever, you can manage and automate every part of the employee lifecycle in a single system. Take onboarding, for example. With Rippling, you can hire a new employee anywhere in the world and set up their payroll, corporate card, computer, benefits, and even third\-party apps like Slack and Microsoft 365—all within 90 seconds. Based in San Francisco, CA, with offices now all over the world, Rippling has raised $1\.2B from the world’s top investors—including Kleiner Perkins, Founders Fund, Sequoia, Greenoaks, and Bedrock—and was named one of America’s best startup employers by Forbes. We prioritise candidate safety. Please be aware that all official communication will only be sent from @Rippling.com addresses. **About the Role** We’re looking for a native Spanish\-speaking Campaigns Manager to drive pipeline generation, database growth, and revenue impact across Southern Europe, starting with Spain. This is a hands\-on, execution\-focused role building multi\-channel campaigns through webinars, lead magnets, newsletters, and virtual events. As the first marketing hire for Spain and Southern Europe, you’ll help establish the foundations of our regional marketing function and grow with it. This is a contract\-based role and can be hybrid, fully remote, or in\-office. You’ll report to the EMEA Head of Demand Generation. **Language Requirements** Native Spanish speaker required. English fluency required. An additional European language is nice to have (but not essential). **What You’ll Do** **Database Growth \& Campaigns** * Build and scale opt\-in acquisition programs through webinars, newsletters, and lead magnets. * Run integrated campaigns across email, digital, events, and direct mail to drive awareness and pipeline. * Test and improve acquisition mechanics—landing pages, registration flows, and value propositions. * Own KPIs for database growth, engagement, and campaign contribution to pipeline. **Localisation \& Reporting** * Create Spanish\-language messaging and content tailored to the Spanish and Southern European market. * Work with sales on targeting, messaging, and campaign priorities. * Localise global programs for the region in line with brand guidelines. * Report on campaign performance and use data to iterate and improve results. **What You’ll Need** * Native Spanish speaker (required); English fluency required * Around 4 years’ experience in campaign management, demand generation, or field marketing in a B2B environment * Experience running webinars, virtual events, newsletters, or lead magnet programs * Comfortable with CRM and marketing automation tools (e.g. Salesforce, Outreach) * Organised, data\-driven, and able to manage multiple campaigns at once * Collaborative and comfortable working cross\-functionally with sales and central teams * Self\-starter with a bias for action in a fast\-paced environment * Additional European language is a plus **Additional Information** Rippling is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce and do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, sexual orientation, veteran or military status, or any other legally protected characteristics. Rippling is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities who need assistance during the hiring process. To request a reasonable accommodation, please email accommodations@rippling.com

title: Marketing Specialist, China Marketing ( China based )
description: **About Agoda** At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world. Today, we are part of Booking Holdings \[NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide. No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us. **About The Role** Agoda is looking for an efficient and analytical Marketing Specialist to join China Marketing Team. The team plays a critical role in Agoda’s ongoing growth by executing and managing the social media ecosystem of Agoda in China market. The Marketing Specialist will be responsible for coordinating and collaborating with both internal and external teams to ensure a successful communication launch. The Marketing Team also fosters a great learning environment. You will be able to learn and grow by working closely with experts from a variety of backgrounds from all over the world. **Some typical activities that you will support are:** * Test, measure, and decide: you will think, plan and collaborate on new test ideas to improve customer engagement, business results, and system performance * Cross\-team collaboration: we are the voice of Agoda to the customers and there are many teams with something to say. You will work with them to understand the goal, design the communication, launch the campaign, and measure the results * Campaign/communication execution: our team manages multiple social media channels and products, and we are always looking for more. You will take ownership of some of those and maintain, monitor, optimize them and, possibly, help us create new ones. **In this role, you’ll get to:** * Learn: From understanding how to improve and streamline the execution to learning technical concepts and details necessary to realize sophisticated ideas, we will help you become super\-efficient and super\-effective. * Deliver value: our biggest constraint is not coming up with new ideas but managing to launch and test always more of them. By helping us with that you will effectively become a multiplier for our team's results. * Test: prove your ideas by A/B testing changes to any of our communication products. Define your hypothesis, design the framework, launch the campaign, and collect and analyze the results of large\-scale, quick delivery tests. Everybody's ideas are welcome and yours are no different. **What you’ll need to succeed:** * 1\-2 years' experience in relevant marketing executionjobs * Great work ethic and detail oriented. You will do whatever it takes to deliver a growth mindset effort * Confidence to express an opinion, speak up your ideas, and manage various stakeholders * Willingness and ability to learn. Not just execute, but also see how you can always become more efficient * Excellent command of Chinese languages (Native is a plus) * Good command of the English language, both written and spoken * title: Mathematics Specialist (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Mathematics Expert (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your deep knowledge of mathematics could directly shape how AI reasons, solves problems, and communicates with millions of people around the world? We're looking for Mathematics Experts — Masters and PhD holders — to help build and refine the mathematical reasoning capabilities of cutting\-edge AI models. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role. You'll work asynchronously alongside world\-class AI research teams, applying your expertise to problems that matter. No prior AI experience needed — just serious mathematical depth and a sharp eye for rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Develop, solve, and review advanced mathematical problems with real\-world relevance and complexity * Design rigorous problem statements across algebra, calculus, statistics, discrete mathematics, or related fields * Evaluate AI\-generated mathematical reasoning for correctness, depth, and clarity * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and domain experts to improve model performance * Ensure mathematical rigor, precision, and intellectual depth across all deliverables * Work independently on task\-based assignments — fully on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Holds a Master's or PhD in Mathematics or a closely related field * Deep expertise in one or more of: algebra, calculus, statistics, or discrete mathematics * Comfortable coding in Python, MATLAB, or Mathematica for research or applied problem\-solving * Exceptionally detail\-oriented with strong analytical and written communication skills * Fluent in English with the ability to articulate complex mathematical concepts clearly * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently without supervision **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, data quality evaluation, or AI training workflows * Background in applied mathematics, mathematical modelling, or computational research * Experience writing or reviewing technical content for academic or professional audiences * Familiarity with AI tools or language model evaluation as an end user **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, intellectually stimulating work * Make a direct, tangible impact on how AI understands and reasons through mathematics * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Mechanical Electrical Engineer
description: **Title:** Mechanical \& Electrical Engineers / Designers (Multiple Roles) **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid options available) **Type:** 12\-Month Contract **Salary:** DOE **Ref:** DES **Overview** We are partnering with a leading engineering consultancy in Dublin to support a range of **complex, high\-tech projects** across semiconductor manufacturing, data centres, and advanced industrial facilities. Due to continued project demand, there are **multiple contract opportunities available** for both Engineers and Designers across Mechanical (HVAC) and Electrical disciplines. These roles offer exposure to the **full project lifecycle** , from concept and design through to construction support, within a highly collaborative, multi\-disciplinary environment. **Open Positions** * Mechanical / HVAC Engineer * Mechanical / HVAC Designer * Electrical Engineer * Electrical Designer **Key Responsibilities (will vary by role)** * Support or lead design activities from concept through to detailed design and construction * Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams (mechanical, electrical, structural, architectural, process) * Develop and review 2D/3D designs using BIM tools such as Revit and AutoCAD * Ensure compliance with industry standards, codes, and project requirements * Contribute to design reviews, risk assessments, and project planning * Support site activities, including surveys and construction observation * Mentor junior team members where applicable **Requirements** * Relevant qualification in Mechanical, Electrical, or Building Services Engineering (or similar) * Experience in design within industrial, semiconductor, data centre, or advanced manufacturing environments * Designers: 3 \- 6\+ years’ experience with strong Revit/AutoCAD skills * Engineers: 5\+ years’ experience with strong design, coordination, and project delivery experience * Strong understanding of HVAC or Electrical systems design (depending on discipline) * Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills **Desirable Experience** * Experience working in BIM environments (Navisworks or similar) * Exposure to large\-scale or technically complex projects * Familiarity with design tools such as ETAP, EasyPower, Carrier HAP, or Trane Trace **Only candidates currently based in Ireland with a valid work visa or EU passport will be considered.** **For more information, contact Corné at 0949316002 or corne@dillones.ie.**

title: Mechanical Engineer
description: **Job Title: Mechanical Engineer Job type: Contract Job Location: Dublin/Onsite** **Company Overview:** Our client is a leading global technology company specializing in telecommunications equipment, consumer electronics, and enterprise solutions. title: Mechanical Foreman
description: Sensori is seeking a proactive Mechanical Foreperson to lead on\-site mechanical activities at our Dublin location. The successful candidate will be a self\-starter who takes ownership of their workload, drives teams to meet deadlines, and maintains high standards for safety and quality. **Key Responsibilities** * Plan and coordinate resources including labour, materials, plant, and subcontractors. * Lead and supervise a mechanical team to deliver projects on time and to specification. * Drive project delivery by managing schedules, priorities, and on\-site workflows. * Enforce safety and quality standards across all site activities. * Liaise with clients, subcontractors, and internal stakeholders to ensure alignment and resolve issues. * Prepare and submit reports on progress, resource usage, and any site incidents. * Monitor and report project progress against milestones and budgets. * Maintain accurate site records and ensure compliance with company procedures. **Required Experience and Skills** * Minimum 5 years’ experience in project management or site supervision within mechanical or mechanical/electrical contracting. * Proven track record of coordinating and delivering medium to large projects. * Trade background with relevant qualifications. * Strong IT skills including Microsoft Word and Excel. * Excellent communication skills in English. * Flexible to travel as project needs require. * Self\-motivated with strong organisational skills and the ability to prioritise under pressure.

title: Mobile Mechanic
description: **Mobile Technician – Full Time** Dublin (Field\-Based) United Tyres Autoservice Job Type: Full\-time, Permanent **Purpose of the Role** Carry out mobile jobs safely and professionally, represent United Tyres Autoservice to a high standard in the field, and make good decisions on when work should be escalated to the workshop or recovery. **Why This Role Matters** Mobile work is often the **first real experience customers have with United Tyres Autoservice**. In this growth phase, the role is critical. Done well, it builds trust, protects vehicle uptime, and ensures the right work flows back into the workshop properly. **Key Responsibilities** * Attend mobile jobs **on time** and complete in\-scope work safely and efficiently * Carry out repairs neatly and to a **high standard** * Capture clear **proof of work**, including photos and notes where required * Maintain **van cleanliness**, stock awareness, and proper equipment care * Communicate clearly with the **Senior Mobile Technician and front desk** when jobs change, fail, or need escalation * Represent United Tyres Autoservice professionally with **customers, drivers, and fleet contacts** **What Success Looks Like** * Strong punctuality and **safe, consistent workmanship** * Clear and professional **job completion and handover discipline** * Smooth hand\-offs when jobs require **workshop support, recovery, or parts** * A well\-maintained van and **reliable field performance** **What We’re Looking For** * Confident carrying out **on\-site repairs** (brakes, discs, batteries, punctures, basic mechanical work) * Good judgment \- knows when a roadside fix should **not be pushed too far** * Strong work ethic, **reliability, and attendance** * Professional attitude with a **calm and customer\-focused approach** Apply via Indeed or contact us directly at: 0872183327 \- Adrian unitedtyreswalkinstown@gmail.com Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €45,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Experience: * Motor Mechanic: 3 years (required) Language: * English (preferred) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Multiple Openings (Associate), Fund/Client Accounting
description: **We welcome you to apply!** When applying to this general posting, our expert BNY Talent Acquisition Team may also review your resume for consideration across other open roles within the company. At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day around the globe, our 50,000\&\#43; employees bring the power of their perspective to the table to create solutions with our clients that benefit businesses, communities and people everywhere. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we help make money work for the world – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team members for the role of **Fund Accountants (Associate)** to join our **Fund Accounting team** . This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways: * Perform accurate and timely delivery of daily NAV calculations in a fast paced and rapidly evolving environment and act as point of escalation for the team for senior / team lead positions * Maintain open lines of communication with key internal teams to ensure accurate delivery of NAV critical inputs, in line with internal service delivery agreements * Critically analyse fund movement and act as point of contact for queries or escalation items from the team within the NAV production environment * Investigate and ensure full close out of operational and audit queries, from both internal and external stakeholders * Manage daily operational requirements across the team, including but not limited to team allocation of duties, annual leave, team performance etc. * Provide key considerations and feedback for initiatives to support change and improvements in alignment with the firm's strategy * Ensure escalation of items requiring urgent resolution to Management Team in a timely manner To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * 3\-7\&\#43; years’ Fund Accounting experience within Operations * Prior experience using Enterprise Invest One is highly preferred * Knowledge of the components of daily NAV calculations and complex financial instruments * Knowledge of Fund Accounting requirements for UK OEICs and/or Luxembourg SICAVs an advantage * Skilled in critically analysing all aspects of NAV Production to identify items requiring further investigation or escalation for senior level positions * Third level degree qualified, ideally in Accounting or Business * Excellent communication skills At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a few of our recent awards: America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2024 World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune, 2024 Human Rights Campaign Foundation, Corporate Equality Index, 100% score, 2023\-2024 Best Places to Work for Disability Inclusion, Disability: IN – 100% score, 2023\-2024 “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2024 Dow Jones Sustainability Indices, Top performing company for Sustainability, 2024 Bloomberg’s Gender Equality Index (GEI), 2023 Our Benefits and Rewards: BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans. This is a talent pipeline posting and does not represent any one particular job opening. By applying to this pipeline requisition, your interest will be reviewed for multiple potential openings based upon your background and disclosed work preference.

title: Network Engineer
description: Want Your Next Big Opportunity as a Network Engineer? I’m Here to Help! **About Your New Employer** * A forward\-thinking leader in technology and consulting services, recognised for its commitment to innovation and employee development. * Be part of a dynamic IT team managing cutting\-edge infrastructure in a global, fast\-paced environment. * Enjoy a supportive culture that values proactive problem\-solving, operational excellence, and personal growth. **What Skills You Need In This Job As A Network Engineer:** * In\-depth experience with Cisco, Nexus, and Brocade networking equipment. * Strong understanding of OSPF, BGP, Layer 2, and Layer 3 protocols. * Proficient with Cisco wireless LAN controller management and troubleshooting. * Solid grasp of network operating systems (JUNOS, Cisco IOS). * Excellent analytical and troubleshooting skills. * Capable of working independently with self\-sustaining motivation. * Strong organisational and mentoring abilities. **About Your New Job As A Network Engineer:** * Independently configure, troubleshoot, and maintain LAN, WAN, Wireless networks, and traffic engineering devices. * Manage, configure, and troubleshoot Cisco Nexus (9K, 7K, 5K, 2K), stack switches, and Brocade devices. * Handle EIGRP and BGP routing, as well as OSPF, and maintain network documentation and SOPs. * Standardise access rules across the environment and implement firewall best practices. * Oversee wireless LAN controllers, access points, and facilitate device refresh projects with a focus on security and manageability. * Address shipping, logistics, and provide user communication/support at local sites. * Proactively monitor infrastructure, anticipate potential issues, and recommend improvements for network performance, capacity, and scalability. **What’s On Offer In This Job As A Network Engineer:** * €500 Daily Rate. * 3 Month Rolling Contract with High Likelihood of Extensions * 4 Day Work Week What’s not to like?! I’m looking forward to chatting with some great Network Engineers.

title: Network Engineer
description: About Us Diacom is a rapidly growing Managed IT Service Provider, delivering enterprise\-grade IT solutions across networking, managed IT services, and voice telecommunications. Our tech stack includes HPE Aruba \& Fortinet networking, Microsoft Windows, Microsoft Azure, and Microsoft 365, and an industry\-leading cybersecurity platform in the MSP IT space. Our growth means we need specialists who can maintain the rapid response times our clients depend on. You’ll be joining a team that values curiosity, has autonomy, and demands technical excellence. The Role The Networks Engineer (L2\) supports the delivery of Diacom’s networking projects, solutions, and services across new and existing customer environments. The role is responsible for the configuration, deployment, maintenance, and troubleshooting of network infrastructure, while ensuring continuity of service, customer satisfaction, and ongoing revenue for the networks vertical of the business. The position also contributes to the development of new service lines, supports the helpdesk with escalations, and maintains high\-quality documentation and technical standards. What You'll Do • Lead the design and deployment of enterprise\-grade network solutions for new and existing customer environments. • Provide expert guidance on network strategy, capacity planning, segmentation, scalability, and security design. • Oversee the maintenance, performance, and reliability of customer network infrastructure, including firewalls, switches, wireless access points, and supporting systems. • Ensure best\-practice configuration, deployment, and lifecycle management of: o Switching platforms (HPE Aruba, Fortinet) o Wireless solutions (HPE Aruba, Fortinet) o Firewalls (Fortinet/FortiGate, Palo Alto) o NAC/AAA platforms (Aruba ClearPass) • Plan, coordinate, and execute scheduled firmware upgrades, maintenance windows, and infrastructure refresh activities. • Act as the senior escalation point for complex and high\-impact network incidents from the Diacom Helpdesk and TAC teams. • Lead root\-cause analysis (RCA), remediation planning, and implementation of long\-term resolution strategies. • Produce detailed reports for internal and external stakeholders covering network health, performance trends, and incident outcomes. • Collaborate with VoIP, Managed Services, and Cybersecurity teams on cross\-functional projects and integrated solutions. • Provide technical input and subject\-matter expertise to sales and account management teams, including solution design and pre\-sales activities. • Provide technical leadership, mentoring, and knowledge\-sharing to junior network engineers. • Contribute to internal technical training, standards development, and process improvement initiatives. • Participate in the after\-hours support roster for critical infrastructure and customer\-impacting events. • Provide availability for overtime during major project deployments or urgent customer requirements as required. What We're Looking For • Exposure to/or experience with enterprise networking technologies (Aruba, Fortinet, Palo Alto). • Full Driver’s License • Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills across wired and wireless networks. • Understanding of NAC principles and AAA frameworks. • Familiarity with network monitoring tools. • Strong communication and customer service orientation. • Ability to produce accurate technical documentation. Nice to Have • Basic understanding of networking concepts, operating systems, and common security controls • Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and communication skills • Ability to work effectively in a team environment and follow established procedures
